https://www.linkedin.com/in/nirmit-kalya.text:
100 Days of Cloud (AWS) – Completed

Today, I successfully completed the 100 Days of Cloud (AWS Track) challenge by KodeKloud.

Over the course of this challenge, I worked hands-on with real AWS infrastructure — focusing on practical implementation rather than just theory.

Key areas I worked on:
- Designing VPC architectures (public & private subnets)
- Configuring Internet Gateways & NAT Gateways
- Implementing secure IAM roles and policies
- Deploying EC2, ALB, and Auto Scaling setups
- S3 static website hosting & bucket security
- RDS provisioning and connectivity
- SQS, SNS, and Lambda integrations
- Infrastructure as Code using CloudFormation
- Log aggregation setups and secure data transfers
- Troubleshooting networking and access issues

This challenge significantly strengthened my understanding of cloud networking, security best practices, scalability patterns, and real-world AWS troubleshooting.

All implementations documented here:

https://www.linkedin.com/in/parth-zinzuv.text:
🌟 A Meaningful Start to 2025: Learning and Growth at MU AIM-2025! 🎉

I got the opportunity to be a part of the Academia Industry Meet (AIM 2025), an event that marked a remarkable start to the new year. Hosted by Marwadi University, it brought together 100+ global leaders from top MNCs to bridge the gap between academia and industry.

It was a privilege for me and my friendto serve as student coordinators for Mr. Mohit Godara and Mr.from. Our interactions with them were very insightful. From learning about the impactful work Autodesk creates to receiving their valuable guidance, these conversations were both memorable and educational.

Additionally, interacting with various industry leaders at the event inspired and encouraged me to aim higher in the year ahead. 🎯

A special thanks to, our respected Pro Vice Chancellor Dr.and our HODfor organizing such an exceptional platform for students like me to network and learn from industry experts.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/niyati-raiya.text:
✨ Industrial Visit to STPI, Pune ✨

On 7th November 2025, we had the wonderful opportunity to visit Software Technology Parks of India, Puneas part of our Computer Engineering Industrial Visit.

The visit was truly insightful and inspiring — we got to explore how STPI supports tech innovation, software exports, and startup incubation across India. It was an amazing experience to understand how real-world software development, infrastructure management, and IT policies come together to drive the tech ecosystem. 💻

During the visit, we also had the chance to interact with professionals who shared their knowledge about the latest technologies, work culture, and the importance of innovation in today’s IT industry. The sessions were highly interactive and gave us a broader perspective on how classroom concepts are applied in real industry scenarios.

A heartfelt thanks to our facultiesMa’am andSir for accompanying us and guiding us throughout the visit. Your encouragement and constant support made this visit even more meaningful. 🙏

Special gratitude to our Head of Department,Sir, for his continuous support, encouragement, and for making such learning experiences possible. 🌟
Also, sincere thanks to our Computer Engineering Department,, for organizing this wonderful and insightful industrial visit.

It was also a day filled with learning, fun, and teamwork — made even more memorable with my amazing friends,,,,,, and.

Such experiences truly remind us how valuable practical learning is in shaping our careers as future engineers! 🚀

#IndustrialVisit#STPI#Pune#ComputerEngineering#LearningExperience#Innovation#Technology#EngineeringJourney#StudentLife#Teamwork#Gratitude#PracticalLearning#TechExposure#FutureEngineer

https://www.linkedin.com/in/akshar-saval.text:
🚀Built: MobileDevPro AI – A Multi-Agent AI Coding Platform
I’m excited to share MobileDevPro AI, a full-stack multi-agent GenAI system designed to generate, fix, and improve code across multiple mobile and backend technologies.
🎯 Purpose
The goal of this project is to simulate how a real AI engineering platform works:
Intelligent agent routing
Tool-based code analysis
Streaming AI responses
Clean frontend + backend integration
It goes beyond simple prompting and focuses on architecture, scalability, and real-world engineering problems.
🧠 What It Can Do
✅ Generate production-ready code in any language
✅ Fix and refactor code for:
Kotlin (Android)
Flutter (Dart)
Swift (iOS)
React Native
✅ Auto-route prompts to the correct agent
✅ Manual agent selection override
✅ Real-time streaming output (ChatGPT-like UX)
✅ Tool-based static analysis using a separate MCP server
✅ Clean HTML/CSS/JS frontend connected via FastAPI
🧩 Tech Stack
LangGraph – agent orchestration & workflow graph
LangChain – LLM integration & prompting
Gemini LLM – code generation & reasoning
FastAPI – backend API layer
MCP (Tool Server) – static code analysis & execution
HTML / CSS / JavaScript – frontend UI
Streaming APIs – real-time token output
🏗️ Architecture Highlights
Multi-agent system with router & validator
Microservice-style MCP tool server
Separate API layer for frontend consumption
Real production debugging (CORS, ports, streaming, envs)
🔗 GitHub Repository
👉
I learned a lot about agent design, system orchestration, streaming AI UX, and full-stack GenAI engineering while building this.
Open to feedback, suggestions, and discussions 🚀

https://www.linkedin.com/in/sundarpichai.text:
We’re introducing new Gemini capabilities across Google Docs, Sheets, Slides and Drive to help you get things done faster. 

You can now allow Gemini to pull relevant information from your files, emails and the web, to connect dots and uncover useful insights.

Here’s what else you can do:

📑 Docs: Go from a blank page to a fully formatted first draft instantly by drawing information from your own files. Plus, use "Match Writing Style" so it doesn’t sound like the doc was edited by committee, even if it was :)

🔢 Sheets: Describe what you need and Gemini will build entire spreadsheets, solve complex problems, and fill in the data 9X faster than if you had to manually enter it yourself. Gemini in Sheets is now #1 on SheetsBench full leaderboard.

🎤 Slides: Generate professional, on-brand layouts and editable diagrams from a simple prompt, no design skills required!

💽 Drive: No more digging through folders to find what you’re looking for. AI Overviews give you summarized, cited answers at the top of your search results (I love this!).

These new features are starting to roll out today in beta, available first to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers in English globally for Google Docs, Sheets and Slides, and in the US for Drive.

See everything that’s coming here:
